### Post by lavinog on 2007-09-25
> **lhabjane said:**
> no, I don't know the exact VGA. I can check in windows. But, how that can help me?

sorry took so long to respond.
It is good to know what you have so that when you have problems with it:
1) other people with the same exact card can give you better information on how to deal with these issues.
2) there may be a issue with your specific vga model and knowing what model it is can help find the work around.

for instance on one computer I installed linux. it had a intel i810 chipset which disabled 3d if you were using 24bit color...the fix was to switch to 16 bit color...i would have never figured this out if i didn't know what video card i had.

what brand computer are you using? if it is something like hp, dell, gateway...etc you should be able to go to their website and put in your model number and find out what card you have.
if it is a generic computer you may be able to use the motherboard model number (if it is an integrated vga card) and go to the motherboard mfg website.
if it is a separate vga card the you should be able to get the information directly off the card.

---
